page_content:
ARCH628 Selected Topics in Architectural History (1-4 Credits) Graduate Selected Topics in Architectural History Prerequisite: ARCH225 and ARCH226; or ARCH425 and ARCH426; or permission of the ARCH-Architecture Program. Restriction: Permission of ARCH-Architecture Program. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.

ARCH629 Graduate Independent Studies in Architectural History (1-4 Credits) Proposed work must have a faculty sponsor and receive approval of the Architecture Program Curriculum Committee. Prerequisite: ARCH225 and ARCH226; or ARCH425 and ARCH426; or permission of the ARCH-Architecture Program. Restriction: Permission of ARCH-Architecture Program. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.

ARCH488 Selected Topics in Architectural Preservation (1-4 Credits) Selected Topics in Architectural Preservation. Restriction: Permission of ARCH-Architecture Program. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.

ARCH635 Seminar in the History of Modern Architecture (3 Credits) Advanced investigation of historical problems in modern architecture. Prerequisite: ARCH225 and ARCH226; or ARCH425 and ARCH426; or permission of the ARCH-Architecture Program.

ARCH489 Independent Studies in Architectural Preservation (1-4 Credits) Proposed work must have a faculty sponsor and receive approval of the Architecture Program Curriculum Committee. Restriction: Permission of ARCH-Architecture Program. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.
